element_introduction_order.element: Basic ship selection and faction choice
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn matchup mechanics and ship strengths/weaknesses across 14 distinct vessels
Resource management through colonies providing currency and crew for ship repairs
Single-screen overhead battles with gravity wells, momentum mechanics, unique ship abilities
Weapons and secondary abilities consume battery; auto-recharge with few exceptions
Limited crew represents total damage; replenished at colonies, creating strategic meta-loop

element_introduction_order.element: Gravity well hazard (planets in combat arena)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Environmental obstacle that can crash ships or provide momentum advantage through proximity
Discovering counter-matchups; weaker ships viable with skilled play despite cost differentiation

typical_tension_triggers: Enemy fleet encountered on map (triggers combat)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Dual-rhythm structure: methodical turn-based strategy interspersed with intense arcade-style real-time combat encounters; tension escalates as fleets converge toward opponent starbase

difficulty_curve.early_game: Players learn faction choice and basic ship matchups through selectable scenarios
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Strategic expansion and resource management complexity increases as fleets move across rotating star map toward objectives
difficulty_curve.late_game: Convergence toward opponent starbase destruction requiring sustained combat victories and crew management
notable_spikes: First mandatory combat encounter (transition from pure strategy to action)
Starbase assault requiring multiple consecutive combat wins with limited crew reserves

notable_level_patterns.description: Each mission presents initial fleet positioning on rotating star map with up to three ship actions per turn; players choose exploration/colonization/fortification actions before enemy encounters trigger combat
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic campaign mission (9 on home computers, 15 on Genesis)

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-screen 1v1 battles triggered by fleet collision; gravity well present as environmental hazard; cockpit animations and sound design provide character differentiation during engagement
Colonies captured during strategy phase provide ongoing currency and crew replenishment; crew damage taken in combat creates incentive to return to friendly colonies rather than push aggressively
